creator: Alexander Terp
description:
RE-VERIFIED 2026-06-14 (rad v0.10.1): still reproduces. (Note: real case syntax is single-line `case X -> body`, not the `case X:` originally shown.)

All case expressions in a switch are evaluated before matching, including those after the matching case. Functions with side effects in case expressions execute for every case.

**Repro:**
fn log_(x):
    print("evaluating: {x}")
    return x

switch 1:
    case log_(1) -> print("matched 1")
    case log_(2) -> print("matched 2")
// prints "evaluating: 1" AND "evaluating: 2", then "matched 1"

**Source:** `core/interpreter.go:451-459` loops through all cases and calls eval(key) on every case key before matching.

**Design:** switch to lazy evaluation (stop after the first match) - a control-flow change.
title: Switch evaluates ALL case expressions eagerly, even after match found
